title: AMQP Queue
description: Schema describing an AMQP 0-9-1 queue. Queues store messages and deliver them to consumers. They can be durable, exclusive, or auto-deleted, and support various arguments for controlling message TTL, length limits, and dead-lettering.

name.description: The name of the queue. An empty string causes the broker to generate a unique name.

durable.description: If true, the queue survives broker restarts.

exclusive.description: If true, the queue is used by only one connection and will be deleted when that connection closes.

autoDelete.description: If true, the queue is automatically deleted when the last consumer unsubscribes.

arguments.description: Optional arguments for queue declaration providing extended configuration.

x-message-ttl.description: Per-queue message time-to-live in milliseconds. Messages older than this are discarded or dead-lettered.

x-expires.description: Queue expiry time in milliseconds. The queue is deleted after being unused for this duration.

x-max-length.description: Maximum number of messages the queue can hold. Overflow behavior is controlled by x-overflow.

x-max-length-bytes.description: Maximum total size in bytes of all messages in the queue.

x-overflow.description: Overflow behavior when x-max-length or x-max-length-bytes is reached.

x-dead-letter-exchange.description: Name of the exchange to which dead-lettered messages are republished.

x-dead-letter-routing-key.description: Routing key to use when dead-lettering messages. If not set, the original routing key is used.

x-max-priority.description: Maximum priority level the queue supports (0-255). Enables priority queuing.

x-queue-mode.description: Queue mode. 'lazy' mode moves messages to disk as early as possible to reduce memory usage.

x-queue-type.description: The queue type. Classic queues are the default; quorum queues provide data safety via replication.
